def: s.f. = phirkī, q.v.

def: v.t. To cause to turn; to cause to walk about, &c. = phirānā, q.v.; to send round, to circulate.
intj. Pish! pshaw! tush!
v.n. To hiss
v.n. To slip, slide; to make a slip, to err
subs: v.n. To slip, &c.; — to incline (to), be attracted (towards).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Slippery, smooth.
adj. Slippery.
s.f. = phislāʼo, q.v.
s.m. Slipping, sliding; slipperiness; erring, slip, error.
v.t. To cause to slip or slide; to cause to err.

def: s.m. Slipping, sliding; slipperiness.
v.n. To be terrified; to hiss; to spit (as a cat).
s.m. Curse, malediction; — intj. Fie! fie upon it! out upon you! a fig for it! a curse on it!
subs: intj. = phiṭ; — phiṭ-phiṭ karnā, v.t. To cry fie or shame on; to rail at, to sneer at, treat with contempt
s.m. Removing (a thing or person) to a distance, driving away; curse, &c. = phiṭ; — phiṭ-kār barasnā, v.n. Curses to be showered down (on), to be accursed
v.t. To cry fie or shame on, to turn from with contempt; to turn away with disdain; to reject; to drive away; to rail at, revile; to curse.

def: v.t. To beat up and mix; to beat up into a froth, &c. = pheṅṭnā, q.v.
v.n. To be beaten up and mixed, to be whipped (as eggs, &c.); — to be wiped off, paid off, discharged (as a debt, &c.).
intj. Faugh! pooh! pshaw! fie!
s.m. Dregs, &c. = phok, q.v.
s.m. Dregs, sediment, feculence, lees, dross, refuse, rubbish, trash, anything worthless (see phokaṛ).
adj. Four (in the language of brokers).
adj. Hollow, not solid; concave; empty; light; soft; — plain, simple, mere (also written phoṅk, phoṅkā); — phok, s.f. Hollow, concavity; notch (of an arrow).
adj. Hollow, not solid; concave; empty; light; soft; — plain, simple, mere (also written phoṅk, phoṅkā); — phok, s.f. Hollow, concavity; notch (of an arrow).

def: adj. Hollow, &c. = phok, q.v.
s.m. = phok, q.v.
s.m. A worthless thing or person; a good-for-nothing or despicable person; an indigent person; — a thing given gratis, a gratuity

adv. Gratuitously, gratis, for nothing ( = saiṅt-meṅ)

def: s.f. Soft or porous soil (which readily yields to the plough).

ety: phok, q.v.+Prk. लो, and लओ = S. ल+कः
def: adj. Hollow, &c. = phok, q.v.

adj. = phaphsā, q.v.

def: v.t. To break, crack, split, burst open, break open, break to pieces, to shatter; to make manifest, to disclose, divulge, betray (a secret)

s.m. A blister; a swelling, intumescence; anything inflated or puffed out.
s.m. = phūhā or phohā, q.v.

adj. & s.f. = phūhaṛ, q.v.

adj. &c.; see next.

adj. & s.m. Rude; foolish; obscene; talking obscenely; — one given to obscene talk.

v.t. = phūṅknā, q.v.
v.n. To be blown, be inflated; to be blown up (a fire), be blown into a flame; to be set fire to, be burnt; to burn, be consumed (lit. and fig.; — also written phuṅknā)

def: s.f. A hollow piece of bamboo used to blow a fire, a blow-pipe; — a firelock or pistol.
s.m. A flower, a blossom; — a boss, knob, stud, bunch of ribands, rosette, cockade; — the menses (of which children are the blossoms)
subs: a swelling; — a spark of fire; lights or fire

(seen at night); the bones of a corpse after partial cremation; — a ceremony performed (by Musalmāns) in honour of a deceased person on the third day after his death, when a portion of the Qorʼan is recited, and flowers are placed on trays in the midst of the assembly); — a white metal, bell-metal, electrum; — the white marks of leprosy; — a pearl or white opaque spot in the corner of the eye ( = phullī); — a maggot that is hatched in meat or in neglected sores; — a head, stick, small quantity (as we say a head of celery, — e.g. bhang-ke do phūl denā); — the best kind of native wine
v.n. The menses to come on, to have the menses

s.f. A knot of ribands, rosette, cockade

s.m. A kind of Hindū sport or show

s.f. The musk melon, Cucumis momordica; — a cucumber run to seed and bursting elastically (as the Cucumis utilissimus, Roxb., and Momordica mixta, (Roxb.).
adj. Arrogant, contemptuous, disdainful.

s.f. Crack, split, break, flaw; opening, gap, breach, fissure, rift; separation; cracked or broken vessels (of metal, or crockery); — difference (of opinion), misunderstanding, discord, dissension, split, quarrel, feud
subs: (-meṅ), Dissension to arise or take place (between or among), to disagree, be divided (in opinion)
(-meṅ), To sow discord (between or among), to set by the ears

ety: perf. part. of phūṭnā; and = Prk. फुट्टिओ = S. स्फुटित+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Broken, cracked, chipped, burst, shivered
subs: adv. With a distorted or wry face.

def: s.f. The expanded hood of a snake.
s.f. = phuṭkī, q.v.
s.f. Racking pain (in the bones or joints = har-phūṭan); — misunderstanding, disagreement, discord, wrangling ( = phūṭ).
v.t. To cause (water, &c.) to boil; — to increase; to promote, &c. (see phūṭnā).
s.f. Disagreement; disparity, &c. ( = phūṭ).
s.f. The inusk melon ( = phūṭ, q.v.).
adj. Odd, separated, &c. = phuṭ-kar, phuṭ; — separate, incurred at different places (as debts), sundry, miscellaneous, odds and ends of.
adj. See s.v. phut.
adj. Odd, &c. = phuṭ, q.v.; — arrogant, over-bearing.
adv. = phūṭ, q.v.
adv. = phūṭ, q.v.
s.f. The tailor-bird, Orthotomus longicauda (cf. phudkī).
s.f. A blot, spot, stain, speck; splash; — the undissolved portions of any soluble matter.
adj. Odd, &c. = phuṭ, q.v.
adj. Cracked, bad, base (coin).
v.n. = phūṭnā, q.v.

ety: phūṭ˚ = Prk. फुट्ट(इ) = S. स्फुट्य(ते), passive of rt. स्फुट्;+nā = anā = Prk.अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To be broken, to be broken into; to be broken down; to be dispersed, be separated, be detached; to separate; to be unpaired; to break, crack, split, burst; to break out or forth; to sprout, shoot, bud, germinate; to burst out or forth, to gush out, to escape (from, -se); to burst out crying; to come into view, to appear, to rise, be spread, be diffused (as a smell, &c.); to be made public, to transpire, to leak out, get wind; to boil (as water); to flourish, get preferment or promotion
subs: v.n. (intensive), To break to pieces, to burst asunder, &c.
v.n. To be broken; to be dispersed; to be unpaired.

def: adv. = phūṭ-phūṭ, q.v.s.v. phūṭnā.
adj. Odd, unpaired, &c. = phuṭ, q.v.
s.f. Small fine rain, drizzle; mist, fog; small spot or drop
subs: v.n. Small drops of rain to fall; to drizzle
adj. Having small spots, marked with small spots, spotted.

def: adj. & s.f. = phūhaṛ, q.v.
s.m. A fountain, a jet-dʼeau ( = fawārā); a watering-can with a rose; the perpendicular tube of a ḥuqqa.
s.f. Fat, &c. = pīh, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = piyā, q.v.
s.f. Pus, matter, purulent discharge (from a sore, &c.)
s.f. Suppuration, mattering.
s.f. = H پيبيانا पीबियाना pībiyānā, v.n. See pibiyānā and pibiyāhaṭ the correct forms).
v.n. To become mattery, to suppurate (a sore, &c.).
v.n. = H پيبياهٿ पीबियाहट pībiyāhaṭ, s.f. See pibiyānā and pibiyāhaṭ the correct forms).
s.f. Water in which rice has been boiled, rice-gruel; gruel ( = pīćh).
s.m. A wrestler.
s.f. Wrestling.

ety: pićak = Prk. पिच्चक्के(इ), or पिच्चक्क(इ), fr. S. पिच्च+कृ
def: v.n. To be pressed or squeezed, to be flattened; to be dinted; to be shrivelled; to be reduced (a swelling).
s.m. The belly or abdomen, the stomach; the back (of an animal).
s.f. The instep; the calf of the leg.
adj. Big-bellied, corpulent.
s.m. = pać-pać, q.v.
s.m. Inflammation of the eyes, opthalmia.
s.m. Feather of a tail (esp. of a peacock); a peacock's tail; a tail; a crest
adj. Slimy, slippery
adj. Ninety-five ( = paćanwe, q.v.).
s.f. A bundle of feathers of a peacock's tail.
s.f. Sauce, gravy; the silk-cotton, Bombax heptaphyllum; the pot-herb Basella rubra and B. lucida; the sisu-tree, Dalbergia sisu; the esculent root Arum Indicum.
s.f. The tree Dalbergia sisu.
adj. See s.v. piććha.
adj. Crushed, jammed, smashed, bruised (cf. paććī).
adj. Eighty-five ( = paćāsī, q.v.).
s.f. Rice-water, &c. = pīć, q.v.

s.f. The scum of boiled rice and of other grain; the gum of the silk-cotton tree; the venomous saliva of a snake; a multitude, heap; the calf of the leg; a sheath, coat or cover; the areca nut, betel nut; a line, row, range; a diseased affection of a horse's feet; a plantain, Musa sapientum; the sisu tree, Dalbergia sisu; armour, a sort of cuirass or jacket.
s.m. The hinder or back part, the rear; following; pursuit; persecution; — absence
subs: v.n. To be pursued; to have (an enemy) in the rear
To turn away, to leave, withdraw
(-kā), To get rid (of), shake off; to get away (from)
(-kā), To give up following, or persecuting; to let (one) alone; to give over, cease
(-kā), To follow, pursue, chase; to press, importune, persecute; — to back (as a horse); to recoil (as a gun, &c.)
(-kā), To stick to, to pursue unremittingly; to persecute

pīćheṅ, adv. & postpn. In the rear (of, -ke); in pursuit (of); behind, astern; after (both in time and space); afterwards, subsequently; back, ago; besides; — in the absence (of); after the death (of); — in consequence (of); on, upon; on account (of); for the sake (of), for
v.n. To come after; to follow; to ensue; to come late
(-ke), To run after, to dance attendance (on); to importune, dun, persecute, torment; to pursue doggedly or tenaciously; to make a point (of); — to drop behind, to be outstripped
adv. (intens. of pīćhe), One after another, in succession; in a train, row, or series
pīćhe rakhnā, v.t. To put or leave behind; to outstrip, surpass; — to lay by, store up
v.n. To remain behind or back, to fall or lag behind; to be left remaining; to survive; — pīćhe rahā-huʼā, adj. Remaining; surviving
adv. From behind; in the rear; — afterwards, subsequently
v.t. To put behind; to cast behind the back; to keep back, to restrain
v.n. To retreat; to depart or withdraw (through fear)
pīćhe lagnā or lag-jānā or lag-rahnā
(-ke), To follow, pursue, prosecute, &c. ( = pīćhā karnā, q.v.)
(-ke), To get or go behind; to follow after; to go in the wake or train (of).

def: adj. Last, &c. = pićhlā, q.v.
subs: s.f. Late cultivation; a crop which has ripened late in the season.

def: adj. Last, &c. = pićhlā, q.v.
subs: s.f. Late cultivation; a crop which has ripened late in the season.

def: adj. Back, &c. = pićhlā, (used in comp.)
subs: s.f. A female goblin with feet turned backward; the ghost of a woman; an ugly, or frightful woman, an ogress.

ety: Prk. पच्छल्ले or पच्छले; S. पक्ष्च+लः
def: adv. (prov.) = pīćhe, q.v.; cf. also pićhlā.
s.m. = phislan, q.v.
v.n. To slip, &c. = phisalnā, q.v.

ety: prob. S. पाश्चात्यं; or from pīćhā, q.v.]
def: s.f. The rear, back, hinder part, hind quarter; the ropes by which a horse's hind legs are tied, heel-ropes; — adv. In the rear, behind, at the back (of, -kī)
subs: v.n. To kick with the hind legs (a horse); to attack the rear (of), to attack in the rear, or from behind.

def: s.f. A load fastened to, or carried on, the back.
s.m. A cloth or sheet worn round the waist, or thrown loosely over the back (syn. ćaddar).
s.m. = pićhaurā, q.v.
s.f. = pićnaurī, q.v.
s.f. A small ćaddar or sheet; a head-kerchief.
adv. See s.v. pīćhā.
s.m. Back, rear, &c. = pićhwāṛā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Back, rear, posterior, dorsal; after, hind, hinder, hindermost; latter, last, late, bygone, past; succeeding, modern; — s.m. Past or old lessons, &c.
subs: s.m. The last watch (of the night)
v.n. To return (from a place) the instant of reaching (it), to retrace one's steps without tarrying
v.n. To retreat; to withdraw from, or back out of, an agreement
s.f. After-thought, a thought or idea that comes too late, misconception.

def: s.m. A follower; issue by a former marriage.
s.m. A follower; issue by a former marriage.
adv. = pīche, q.v.s.v. pīćhā.
adv. Behind; afterwards; — s.m. Back of a house, &c. ( = pićhwāṛā).
adv. (prov.) = pīćhe, q.v.
s.m. The hinder part (particularly of a saddle).
adj. & s.m. Hinder, hindmost, hindermost; — the rear, rearward, the back part, hind quarter; court or area behind a house, back-yard
subs: adv. At the back, in the rear; abaft, aft, astern.

def: s.f. = pićhwāṛā, q.v.
v.t. To press together, press flat, to squeeze; to shrivel, wrinkle; to burst; to cause to sink or go down; to reduce a swelling.
s.f. A squirt, syringe; clyster, enema
subs: (-ko), To give a clyster or enema (to)
(-ko), To squirt, discharge water, &c. at from a syringe.

ety: pīć˚ = Prk. पिच्छ(इ) or पेच्छ(इ) = S. प्रेक्ष्य(ति), future of rt. पिष्
def: v.t. To trample upon, to crush; to tread down.
s.m. Garden-ground, &c. in the rear of a tank.
s.m. See پچو piću.
s.m. The fruit of the Capparis aphylla (syn. karīl).
s.m. The plant Vangueria spinosa.
s.m. = paćukā; pićkārī, qq.v.
s.m. Cotton; the tamarisk, Tamarix Indica; the plant Barringtonia acutangula; a kind of cormorant or sea-crow.
s.m. See s.v. piću.
s.m. The brown-backed Indian robin, Thamnobia cambagensis.
s.f. = piddā, q.v.
s.m. Covering, shutting; concealment; cover, lid, sheath, wrapper, cloak.

s.f. Stone-chats and wheat-ears (the Saxicolinœ of naturalists, which visit India in the cold season and migrate westward and northward to breed); tomtit; (fig.) a little bit of a thing, an insignificant person, a weak or feeble person.
s.f. A tomtit ( = pidṛī; cf. also piddī).
v.n. = pighalnā, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi पघिलना (paghilanā), from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀧𑀖𑀺𑀮𑀤𑀺 (*paghiladi), from Sanskrit *प्रघिरति (*praghirati), from प्र- (pra-, “forward, forth”) + घृ (ghṛ, “to moisten”). Cognate with Punjabi پِگَھلݨا (pighalṇā) / ਪਿਘਲਣਾ (pighlaṇā), Marathi पाघळणे (pāghaḷṇe).

def: v.t. = pighlānā, q.v.
v.n. To melt,to be melted, to dissolve; to be in fusion, to fuse, to flow (as a metal); to melt, be softened, moved, or touched; to yield, give way.
v.t. To cause to be melted, &c.; to melt, &c. = pighlānā.

ety: caus. of pighalnā; pighlā˚ = S. प्रगालय˚
def: v.t. To melt, dissolve, fuse ( = ṭighlānā), to smelt or cast (metal); to soften, assuage, mollify (an angry or a hard person).
s.m. Melting, fusion, flux, liquefaction ( = piglāṭ).
v.t. = pighlānā, q.v.
s.f. Melting, fusion, liquefaction ( = pighlāʼo).
s.f. Fat, grease, tallow.
s.m. A plug for closing the mouth of a granary.
s.m. 'Father's home'; wife's father's house or family (syn. maʼikā).
s.m. A flea ( = pissū).
s.m. A kind of bird (prob. a cuckoo; cf. pik).
s.m. The Indian cuckoo, Cuculus indicus
subs: (pika + aksha), s.m. 'Cuckoo's eye'; a vegetable and perfume (com. called roćanī)
(˚ka+an˚), s.m. A small bird (com. called ćāṭakīya)
s.m. 'The cuckoo's friend'; the mango tree
pik-bayanī = pik-vayanī, adj. 'Having a voice like the cuckoo,' an epithet of women; — s.f. A cuckoo-voiced woman.

ety: Prk. पिक्का, or पिक्कं; S. पक्का, or पक्कं; cf. also pūg
def: s.f. The juice of the betel leaf chewed and spit out
subs: s.m. pīk-dānī, s.f. A vessel for holding the pīk; a spittoon (syn. ugāl-dān).

def: s.m. The Indian cuckoo ( = pik, q.v.).
s.f. = phīk, q.v.
